fact_nuggets.text: Super Luck (Japanese: Good Luck) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV. In Generation IX, it is the signature Ability of Murkrow and Honchkrow.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Improves the chances of the Pokemon making critical hits. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD The Pokemon's moves have a high critical-hit rate! MD RT DX The Pokemon's moves have a high critical-hit rate.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation IV 1.2.2 Generation VIII 1.2.3 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Super Luck increases the critical hit ratio by 1 stage. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon HeartGold and SoulSilver only, if a Pokemon with Super Luck breaks a rock and finds an item, the game will instead pick the item below it in the game's list, unless it was the 5% item (this effectively means it will never find the most common item). Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] If a Pokemon with this Ability leads the party (even if fainted ), the chances of finding a wild Pokemon holding an item increase from 50%/5% to 60%/20%. This effect is shared with Compound Eyes . Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Scarlet and Violet , Super Luck has no effect on the items held by wild Pokemon.
In battle [ edit | edit source ] Super Luck increases the critical hit ratio by 1 stage.
Raises moves' critical hit rates one stage.
